message:
refactor(cli): richer ID entropy, unicode-aware truncation, SQL-level label filtering, and cleanup

create.rs:
- Pass description and creator fields to NewIdInput when generating
  issue IDs. Previously both were hardcoded to None, which meant the
  hash entropy came solely from the title. Including the description
  and actor username reduces collision probability for issues with
  similar titles.
- Remove two redundant `continue` statements at the end of retry
  loops (clippy::needless_continue equivalent).
- Replace unwrap_or_else fallback for DependencyType::parse with
  expect(), since the FromStr impl is infallible (unknown strings
  map to DependencyType::Custom).

graph.rs:
- Import unicode_width::UnicodeWidthStr and use visual width (not
  byte length) when deciding whether to truncate titles in the
  all-graph view. This prevents mid-character truncation for CJK
  and emoji titles. Delegates to crate::format::truncate_title()
  for consistent truncation with ellipsis.

history.rs:
- Convert nested if-let + kind check to idiomatic let-chain for
  the remove_file error path in restore_backup().

list.rs:
- Remove label and label_any from needs_client_filters(). These
  filters are now handled at the SQL level by the storage layer,
  so checking them client-side was redundant double-filtering.
- Update unit tests: label-only args no longer trigger client
  filtering; add desc_contains test to confirm it still does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
